strategy: Voracious Void raid boss (40,000 health, 100% base resist), so coordinate a full team around its Roshambo school phases.
Track the school it announces each cycle (it switches every 3 rounds) — it dictates which charms, wards, DoTs, and HoTs are usable or countered, so adapt your spell types to the current phase.

Starve its Scion Power Nova at the end of round 3 of each cycle: each phase has a power-up condition you must NOT feed (e.g. don't stack 2+ DoTs on a wizard in Fire phase, don't put 4+ wards on it in Ice phase, don't leave 4+ charms on it in Storm phase).

Lean on the inspired casts unlocked here (Shadow Surge, Retribution, Brush Fire, Dismal Seraph, Fabled Revenant, Levin Bruin, Rotting Fomori, War Oracle, Monumental), since your normal schools are fully resisted.
It self-buffs Power Current every round, so keep the fight moving; handle its elite minion per that minion's school Roshambo counter.
Round 1 everyone is forced to pass, so plan your opening from round 2.
cheatNotes: You can cast Shadow Surge and Retribution in this fight — build around them.
Inspired casts (Brush Fire, Dismal Seraph, Fabled Revenant, Levin Bruin, Rotting Fomori, War Oracle, Monumental) are available — your main damage since normal schools are fully resisted.
Round 1 it forces every wizard to pass — you can't act, so plan your opening from round 2.
It stacks Power Current on itself every round, so its damage climbs — keep the fight moving.

It announces a school and switches every 3 rounds under Roshambo rules, then at the end of round 3 of each cycle casts a Scion Power Nova whose strength depends on the phase condition — track the school and adapt.
In Fire phase, don't let 2+ DoTs sit on any wizard — that powers up its Scion Nova. Clear damage-over-time.
In Ice phase, don't put 4+ positive wards on the Nullity — that powers up its Scion. Avoid warding it.
In Storm phase, don't let 4+ positive charms sit on the Nullity — steal or avoid feeding it charms.
In Myth phase, keep negative wards on wizards under 4 — 4+ powers its Scion. Cleanse them.
In Life phase, don't let 2+ heal-over-time effects sit on the Nullity — deny it HoTs.
In Death phase, keep negative charms on wizards under 4 — 4+ powers its Scion. Cleanse them.

Its elite minion follows Roshambo too, countering specific spell types by its school (Fire counters wards, Ice counters charms, Storm counters DoT/HoT, Myth counters negative charms and positive wards, Death counters positive charms and HoT, Life counters negative wards and DoT, Balance counters when you hold over 9 pips) — mind the minion's school before you cast.
